title: tetragonia expansa var. cornuta
intro: Tetragonia expansa var. cornuta, also known as New Zealand spinach or Cook's cabbage, is a perennial succulent plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Tetragonia expansa var. cornuta include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and providing essential nutrients like iron and calcium. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used as a leafy green vegetable in salads and cooking.
